meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kits to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no portfolio' trap by using SaaS starter kits. Learn an advanced workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship client-ready apps fast.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing clients with empty hands. Leverage a professional SaaS starter kit to build a high-fidelity, functional MVP in hours, not weeks. By combining Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you can deploy a premium, production-ready portfolio piece that commands high-ticket freelance rates immediately.
content.intro:
The silence of a blank portfolio is deafening. You have the technical chops to build world-class software, but without a proven track record, clients treat your proposals like spam. The industry doesn't care about your potential; it cares about the shipping velocity and the polish of your last deployment.

It is time to stop building 'to-do' lists and start shipping SaaS. By utilizing a high-performance starter kit, you bypass the boilerplate hell that stalls most developers. We are going to weaponize Antigravity and Claude Code to turn your lack of a portfolio into a showcase of architectural mastery.

steps.title: Define the Architecture
steps.description: Identify the core SaaS pillars—Auth, Database, and Payments—and use Antigravity to generate a secure, scalable foundation that mirrors industry standards.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a production-ready SaaS starter kit using Next.js 15, Supabase for backend, and Stripe for billing. Implement role-based access control and a clean, modular folder structure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Initialize the repository with the generated Antigravity architecture and verify all environment variables are correctly mapped.'

steps.title: Implement High-Conversion UI
steps.description: Use a design system to ensure your portfolio project looks like a million-dollar SaaS, not a hobby project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a landing page and dashboard layout using Tailwind CSS and Shadcn UI components. Ensure mobile responsiveness and a dark mode toggle.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Apply the Tailwind theme provided by Antigravity and refine component spacing for a professional look.'

steps.title: Integrate Identity and Payments
steps.description: Handle the heavy lifting of user management and subscription logic automatically to demonstrate backend competence.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Integrate Auth.js for GitHub OAuth and set up Stripe webhook handlers for subscription lifecycle management.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Set up the Stripe webhook listener in the API route and document the Auth.js session handling logic.'

steps.title: Automate Data Persistence
steps.description: Build a robust CRUD interface that proves you can handle complex state and database interactions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a database schema for user-specific data storage and implement server actions to handle data mutations with Zod validation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Generate Zod schemas and server actions for all data entities defined in the initial architecture.'

steps.title: Final Polish and Deployment
steps.description: Clean up the code, add documentation, and push to production to finalize your new portfolio showpiece.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add README documentation, linting configuration, and a CI/CD pipeline script for Vercel deployment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Run linting, fix any remaining warnings, and prepare the project for production deployment on Vercel.'

pro_tips: Always include a 'Changelog' page in your portfolio projects to show clients you understand the reality of continuous delivery.
Use Claude Code to generate unit tests for your critical API paths; it signals to clients that you prioritize reliability over speed.
Don't build everything; build the 'money paths'—the authentication flow and the checkout flow are what clients actually care about.
Keep your repository private until the final build is ready, then open-source the UI components to demonstrate your contribution to the community.
Embed a 'Lighthouse' performance report summary in your README to prove your code is optimized for SEO and speed.
content.next_steps: Once your first project is live, iterate on the starter kit to include a custom 'Client Portal' module, then start reaching out to prospects with a link to your live demo instead of a standard resume.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped with the same tools top-tier agencies use to ship enterprise-grade software in record time. Don't let another day pass without a portfolio that speaks for your potential. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
